page: Microsoft Azure E112ibs_v5 is an isolated, block-storage-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EIBSv5 server family. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with an x86_64 architecture, this server features 112 dedicated vCPUs derived from 56 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It operates at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz and is equipped with 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding 6.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes 48 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES cryptography extensions. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance, meaning all storage operations rely on external premium block storage.

In benchmark testing, the E112ibs_v5 demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities and memory performance. The multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests place this instance in the top 25% of evaluated servers, supported by a strong Passmark CPU score of 81,430.47. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 5,839.55 GB/sec for cached reads and 335.8 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Cryptographic performance is particularly notable, with the Passmark CPU encryption test ranking in the top 10% tier. Database operations and Redis SET operations also register strong performance, achieving 96.47 million operations per second in Redis tests. Single-core performance and LLM inference speeds for small and medium models remain in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E112ibs_v5 offers a high-density resource profile suitable for enterprise workloads that require isolated environments and high memory-to-vCPU ratios without the overhead of local physical disks. The absence of local storage and GPU resources makes it a cost-effective choice for memory-intensive applications that rely entirely on network-attached premium block storage. This server is highly suited for large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, multi-threaded data decompression, high-throughput static web serving, and enterprise software compilation.
description: Microsoft Azure E112ibs_v5 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine from the EIBSv5 family, featuring 112 dedicated vCPUs and 672.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, this x86_64 server lacks local storage and GPU hardware, relying instead on premium block storage. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core processing, high memory bandwidth, and top-tier cryptographic performance, with Passmark encryption scores in the top 10%. The server excels in memory-intensive and multi-threaded tasks, showing strong results in Redis operations and database benchmarks. It is highly suited for enterprise databases, in-memory caching, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E112ibs_v5 is an isolated virtual machine featuring 112 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 672 GB of RAM, and strong multi-core and cryptographic performance, optimized for premium block storage workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E112ibs_v5 is an isolated 112-vCPU Intel-based server with 672 GB RAM, optimized for premium block storage and memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An isolated, high-vCPU server featuring massive memory capacity and strong multi-core performance optimized for premium network-attached block storage.
bullet_points: 112 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 672.0 GB of system memory
Isolated virtual machine environment running on Microsoft Hyper-V
Strong multi-core processing and top-tier cryptographic performance
Optimized for premium block storage with no local disk overhead
Best suited for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and enterprise workloads
